데살로니가전서 2:1.content: For you yourselves know, brothers, our visit to you wasn’t in vain,

데살로니가전서 2:2.content: but having suffered before and been shamefully treated, as you know, at Philippi, we grew bold in our God to tell you the Good News of God in much conflict.

데살로니가전서 2:3.content: For our exhortation is not of error, nor of uncleanness, nor in deception.

데살로니가전서 2:4.content: But even as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the Good News, so we speak: not as pleasing men, but God, who tests our hearts.

데살로니가전서 2:5.content: For neither were we at any time found using words of flattery, as you know, nor a cloak of covetousness (God is witness),

데살로니가전서 2:6.content: nor seeking glory from men (neither from you nor from others), when we might have claimed authority as apostles of Christ.

데살로니가전서 2:7.content: But we were gentle among you, like a nursing mother cherishes her own children.

데살로니가전서 2:8.content: Even so, affectionately longing for you, we were well pleased to impart to you, not the Good News of God only, but also our own souls, because you had become very dear to us.

데살로니가전서 2:9.content: For you remember, brothers, our labor and travail; for working night and day, that we might not burden any of you, we preached to you the Good News of God.

데살로니가전서 2:10.content: You are witnesses with God how holy, righteously, and blamelessly we behaved ourselves toward you who believe.

데살로니가전서 2:11.content: As you know, we exhorted, comforted, and implored every one of you, as a father does his own children,

데살로니가전서 2:12.content: to the end that you should walk worthily of God, who calls you into his own Kingdom and glory.

데살로니가전서 2:13.content: For this cause we also thank God without ceasing, that when you received from us the word of the message of God, you accepted it not as the word of men, but as it is in truth, the word of God, which also works in you who believe.

데살로니가전서 2:14.content: For you, brothers, became imitators of the assemblies of God which are in Judea in Christ Jesus; for you also suffered the same things from your own countrymen, even as they did from the Jews

데살로니가전서 2:15.content: who killed both the Lord Jesus and their own prophets, and drove us out, and don’t please God, and are contrary to all men,

데살로니가전서 2:16.content: forbidding us to speak to the Gentiles that they may be saved, to fill up their sins always. But wrath has come on them to the uttermost.

데살로니가전서 2:17.content: But we, brothers, being bereaved of you for a short season, in presence, not in heart, tried even harder to see your face with great desire,

데살로니가전서 2:18.content: because we wanted to come to you—indeed, I, Paul, once and again—but Satan hindered us.

데살로니가전서 2:19.content: For what is our hope, or joy, or crown of rejoicing? Isn’t it even you, before our Lord Jesus at his coming?

데살로니가전서 2:20.content: For you are our glory and our joy.
